---
title: "YAML configuration reference"
format: html
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{YAML configuration reference}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

This reference documents all configuration options available in the `docstyle:` section of `_quarto.yml`. For tutorials on using these options, see [Getting started](tutorial-getting-started.html) and [Round-trip workflow](how-to-roundtrip.html).

## Quick reference

```yaml
docstyle:
  # Styling (includes page layout via @page rules)
  css: styles.css                    # Single file or array of files

  # Round-trip metadata
  source-docx: "source/original.docx"
  imported: "2025-12-20"
  sidecar-dir: _docstyle

  # Output
  output-name: "MyDocument"          # Filename without extension
  output-dir: output

  # Header/footer
  header: { enabled: false }
  footer: { enabled: true, left: "Title", right: "Page {page} of {pages}" }

  # Document elements
  toc: { title: "Contents", levels: "1-2" }
  version-history: { title: "Version history", widths: "15,70,15" }
  author-plate: { show-email: true }

  # Post-render validators (opt-in; error | warn | false)
  validators:
    docx: { no-docstyle-cite-markers: error }
    jats: { well-formed: error, abstract-present: error }
    pdf:  { tagged: error }

  # Author metadata
  authors: [...]
  affiliations: [...]
```

(The abstract uses the standard top-level `abstract:` front-matter key, not a `docstyle:` sub-key — see [Abstract](#abstract).)

## Styling options

### `css`

Path to CSS file(s) defining typography and colours. Later files override earlier ones (layered styles).

```yaml
# Single file
docstyle:
  css: styles.css

# Multiple files (layered)
docstyle:
  css:
    - base-styles.css
    - draft-overrides.css     # Overrides base-styles.css
```

Paths are relative to `_quarto.yml`. CSS selectors map to Word styles:

| CSS Selector | Word Style |
|--------------|------------|
| `h1` | Heading 1 |
| `h2` | Heading 2 |
| `p` | Body Text |
| `.abstract` | Abstract (custom) |
| `.footer` | Footer |

CSS files can also include `@page` rules for page layout configuration. See [Page layout](#page-layout) below.

## Round-trip metadata

These options track document provenance for Word ↔ Quarto workflows.

### `source-docx`

Path to the original Word document this QMD was imported from.

```yaml
docstyle:
  source-docx: "source/ORIGINAL_V1.0.docx"
```

### `imported`

Date when the document was imported (ISO format).

```yaml
docstyle:
  imported: "2025-12-20"
```

### `sidecar-dir`

Directory for metadata files (comments.json, revisions.json, field-codes.json, reference.docx).

```yaml
docstyle:
  sidecar-dir: _docstyle    # Default
```

This directory typically contains:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `comments.json` | Comment metadata (author, date, content) |
| `revisions.json` | Track changes metadata |
| `field-codes.json` | Zotero field codes for citations |
| `references.json` | CSL-JSON bibliography |
| `reference.docx` | Generated style template |
| `reference.docx.hash` | Hash for cache invalidation |

## Output options

### `output-name`

Filename for rendered document (without extension).

```yaml
docstyle:
  output-name: "POPCORN_Protocol_V1.0"
```

If omitted, uses the QMD filename.

### `output-dir`

Directory for rendered output. Usually set at project level:

```yaml
project:
  output-dir: output
```

## Page layout

Page layout is configured via CSS `@page` rules in your stylesheet, not YAML. This follows the CSS Paged Media specification.

```css
/* In styles.css */
@page {
  size: letter;              /* letter | a4 | legal */
  margin: 1in;               /* or individual margins */

  /* Optional: line numbers for manuscript review */
  --docstyle-line-numbers: every 1;        /* every N | none */
  --docstyle-line-numbers-restart: page;   /* page | section | continuous */
  --docstyle-line-numbers-distance: 0.25in;
}

/* Optional: different settings for first page */
@page :first {
  --docstyle-line-numbers: none;
}
```

### Supported `@page` properties

| Property | Values | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| `size` | `letter`, `a4`, `legal` | Optionally add `landscape` or `portrait` |
| `margin` | CSS length | Shorthand (1-4 values) or individual margins |
| `margin-top`, `margin-right`, etc. | CSS length | Individual margins |
| `--docstyle-line-numbers` | `every N`, `none` | Line numbering |
| `--docstyle-line-numbers-restart` | `page`, `section`, `continuous` | When to restart numbering |
| `--docstyle-line-numbers-distance` | CSS length | Distance from text |
| `--docstyle-suppress-top-spacing` | `true`, `false` | Suppress before-spacing on first paragraph after section break |

**Supported units:** `in`, `cm`, `mm`, `pt`

### Named page styles (section breaks)

For content that needs different page settings mid-document (e.g., landscape tables), define named `@page` rules:

```css
/* Default page */
@page {
  size: letter;
  margin: 1in;
}

/* Landscape section for wide tables */
@page landscape {
  size: letter landscape;
  margin: 0.5in;
}

/* Narrow margins for appendices */
@page compact {
  size: letter;
  margin: 0.5in;
}
```

**Usage in QMD:**

```markdown
Normal portrait content here.

{{< pagebreak >}}

::: {.section-landscape}
### Wide table

| Col A | Col B | Col C | Col D | Col E | Col F |
|-------|-------|-------|-------|-------|-------|
| data  | data  | data  | data  | data  | data  |
:::

{{< pagebreak >}}

::: {.section-default}
:::

Back to portrait orientation.
```

The `.section-{name}` class maps to the `@page {name}` CSS rule. The section marker inserts a section break that ends the previous section. Use `{{< pagebreak >}}` before the marker if you want a page break at that point.

## Header and footer

### `header`

Page header configuration.

```yaml
docstyle:
  header:
    enabled: true
    content: "{title}"        # Can include {title}, {date}, {version}
    align: right              # left | center | right
    first-page: false         # Suppress on first page
    style: header             # CSS class for styling
```

### `footer`

Page footer configuration. Supports positional content (left/center/right) or single content.

```yaml
# Positional footer
docstyle:
  footer:
    enabled: true
    first-page: false         # No footer on title page
    style: footer
    left: "Document Title"
    center: ""
    right: "Page {page} of {pages}"

# Simple centered footer
docstyle:
  footer:
    enabled: true
    content: "Page {page}"
    align: center
```

**Available placeholders:**

| Placeholder | Field code | Description |
|-------------|------------|-------------|
| `{page}` | `PAGE` | Current page number |
| `{pages}` | `NUMPAGES` | Total page count (entire document) |
| `{section-pages}` | `SECTIONPAGES` | Page count for the current section only |
| `{title}` | — | Document title (static text) |
| `{date}` | — | Current date (static text) |

Use `{section-pages}` with `page-start` when you want the denominator to count only the pages in one section (e.g., "Page 1 of 2" in an appendix) rather than the entire document.

**Per-section overrides:** Footer content can be overridden per section using div attributes. See [Headers and footers](how-to-headers-footers.html) for recipes and examples.

```markdown
::: {.section-appendix footer-left="Appendix" footer-right="Page {page}"}
Content with a section-specific footer.
:::
```

**Per-section line numbers:** Line numbering can also be customised per section:

```markdown
::: {.section-body line-numbers="page" line-numbers-count-by="5"
     line-numbers-start="1" line-numbers-distance="0.5in"}
Body content with line numbers every 5 lines.
:::

::: {.section-appendix line-numbers="none" footer="false"}
Appendix without line numbers or footer.
:::
```

| Attribute | Values | Description |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `line-numbers` | `continuous`, `page`, `section`, `none` | Restart mode |
| `line-numbers-count-by` | Integer (default: 1) | Show number every N lines |
| `line-numbers-start` | Integer | Starting line number |
| `line-numbers-distance` | CSS length (default: 0.25in) | Distance from text edge |
| `suppress-top-spacing` | `true`, `false` | Suppress top margin on first paragraph after section break (overrides CSS `@page` setting) |

Setting `footer="false"` on a section div suppresses the footer for that section (writes an empty footer file rather than inheriting from the previous section).

Setting `suppress-top-spacing="true"` removes the before-spacing from the first content paragraph after the section break, eliminating the gap that heading styles with `margin-top` create at the top of a page.

## Table of contents

### `toc`

Configures the TOC field inserted by `toc-field.lua` filter.

```yaml
docstyle:
  toc:
    title: "Table of contents"
    title-level: 1            # Heading level for title (1 = Heading1)
    levels: "1-2"             # Include Heading1 and Heading2
    page-numbers: true
    hyperlinks: true
    tab-leader: none          # none | dots | underline
```

**Usage in QMD:**

```markdown
::: toc
:::
```

The `toc-field.lua` filter replaces this placeholder with a Word TOC field code.

## Version history

### `version-history`

Configures the version history table inserted by `version-history.lua` filter.

```yaml
docstyle:
  version-history:
    title: "Version history"
    title-level: 1
    widths: "15,70,15"        # Column percentages: Version, Description, Date
    style: "table-grid"       # table-grid | table-formal
```

**Usage in QMD front matter:**

```yaml
version-history:
  - version: "1.0.0"
    date: "2025-12-20"
    description: "Final release"
  - version: "0.9.0"
    date: "2025-12-01"
    description: "Initial draft for review"
```

**Usage in QMD body:**

```markdown
::: version-history
:::
```

**Table styles:**

| Style | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `table-grid` | All borders visible |
| `table-formal` | Top and bottom borders only |

## Author plate

### `author-plate`

Configures the author list inserted by `author-plate.lua` filter.

```yaml
docstyle:
  author-plate:
    corresponding-marker: "*"
    equal-marker: "†"
    show-orcid: false
    show-email: true
```

**Usage in QMD:**

```markdown
::: author-plate
:::
```

## Abstract

The abstract content comes from the standard top-level `abstract:` front-matter key (the same key Quarto uses) — there is no `docstyle:` sub-key for it. The docstyle-specific part is an optional placeholder div that controls **where** the abstract renders in `docstyle-docx` output.

```yaml
---
title: "My Document"
abstract: |
  Background, methods, and findings in brief.
---
```

**Usage in QMD body:**

```markdown
::: docstyle-abstract
:::
```

Place the `::: docstyle-abstract :::` div where you want the abstract to appear — typically just below `::: author-plate :::`, matching the order the Typst/PDF output already produces. Pandoc's Word writer otherwise hoists the abstract to the very top of the document, above the formatted author plate; the placeholder relocates it.

- **docx only.** The placeholder is a no-op in Typst and JATS, which render `abstract:` natively in their own title areas. The abstract therefore stays in front-matter metadata and serves every format from one source.
- **Opt-in.** Without the div, the abstract renders at the document top (unchanged behaviour). A plain `## Abstract` heading with body prose is also left untouched.
- **Round-trips.** On harvest (`docx_to_qmd()`), the relocated abstract is restored to the `abstract:` front-matter key and an empty `::: docstyle-abstract :::` placeholder is re-emitted at its position.

## Authors and affiliations

### `authors`

Author metadata following Quarto's scholarly metadata schema.

```yaml
docstyle:
  authors:
    - name:
        given: "Jane"
        family: "Smith"
      orcid: "0000-0001-2345-6789"
      email: "jane@example.org"
      corresponding: true
      equal-contributor: true
      affiliations:
        - ref: inst1
        - ref: inst2
      roles:
        - conceptualization
        - "writing – original draft"
```

### `affiliations`

Institution definitions referenced by authors.

```yaml
docstyle:
  affiliations:
    - id: inst1
      name: "University of Example"
      department: "Department of Research"
      city: "Ottawa"
      region: "Ontario"
      country: "Canada"
    - id: inst2
      name: "Research Institute"
      city: "Toronto"
      country: "Canada"
```

## Complete example

**CSS file** (`styles.css`):

```css
/* Page layout */
@page {
  size: letter;
  margin: 1in;
}

/* Typography */
body {
  font-family: "Arial", sans-serif;
  font-size: 11pt;
}

h1 {
  font-size: 14pt;
  font-weight: bold;
}
```

**YAML configuration** (`_quarto.yml`):

```yaml
project:
  type: default
  output-dir: output
  pre-render: _docstyle/pre-render.R
  post-render: _docstyle/post-render.R

format:
  docstyle-docx:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false

execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false

docstyle:
  css:
    - base-styles.css
    - manuscript-draft.css

  source-docx: "source/original.docx"
  imported: "2025-12-20"
  sidecar-dir: _docstyle

  header:
    enabled: false

  footer:
    enabled: true
    first-page: false
    style: footer
    left: "My Document"
    right: "Page {page} of {pages}"

  toc:
    title: "Table of contents"
    title-level: 1
    levels: "1-2"
    page-numbers: true
    hyperlinks: true
    tab-leader: none

  version-history:
    title: "Version history"
    title-level: 1
    widths: "15,70,15"
    style: "table-grid"

  author-plate:
    corresponding-marker: "*"
    show-email: true

  authors:
    - name:
        given: "Jane"
        family: "Smith"
      email: "jane@example.org"
      corresponding: true
      affiliations:
        - ref: inst1

  affiliations:
    - id: inst1
      name: "Research University"
      city: "Ottawa"
      country: "Canada"
```

## Validators

Opt-in post-render checks that inspect rendered outputs and fail the render on silent failures. Configure under `docstyle.validators.<format>`; each validator is `error` (fail the render), `warn` (print to stderr, continue), or `false`/off.

```yaml
docstyle:
  validators:
    docx:
      no-docstyle-cite-markers: error   # unreplaced DOCSTYLE_CITE:: markers in the docx
    jats:
      well-formed: error                # JATS XML parses
      abstract-present: error           # an <abstract> element exists
    pdf:
      tagged: error                     # PDF carries a structure tree (Tagged: yes)
```

| Validator | Format | Catches |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| `no-docstyle-cite-markers` | docx | `DOCSTYLE_CITE::` markers left in `word/document.xml` — the post-render Zotero injection silently skipped (often renv shadowing). |
| `well-formed` | jats | Rendered JATS XML does not parse. |
| `abstract-present` | jats | No `<abstract>` element — e.g. a `# Abstract` body heading landed as a `<sec>` in `<body>` instead of `<front>`, which PMC drops. |
| `tagged` | pdf | PDF has no structure tree. Bare-minimum PDF/UA precondition (not full UA-1 conformance, which needs veraPDF). |

The registry is plugin-style (`R/validate_output.R`); a new validator is a `function(path) -> list(pass, message)` plus one registry entry. Run ad-hoc with `validate_docstyle_output()`.

## See also

- [CSS property reference](reference-css-properties.html): Every supported CSS property and its Word equivalent
- [File conventions](reference-file-conventions.html): Sidecar files and directory structure
- [Architecture](architecture.html): How docstyle processes configuration
- [CSS to Word](css-to-word.html): Tutorial on creating Word templates from CSS
